feed.title: SAP Community - SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central
feed.subtitle: SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: CC group not receiving workflow emails
entry.content:
workflow does not have approvers. It only has CC users and Employee & Manager are set up to receive a custom email. However emails are not getting trigger

 

Any help - thank you!

entry.title: Dyanmic Breaks and Gaps in Working Time
entry.content:
Hi,

We are making use of dynamci breaks. The break gets deducted when an employee works more than 5.5 hours. 

Gaps in working time are counted towards break time. 

Currently if the employee takes a 15 minute before (5.5 hours into his shift) the dynamci break deducts the additional 15 minutes correctly. 

However, when an employee takes their 30 minute break after 5.5 hours into their shift, the shift automatically deducts the full 30 minutes (even though he has a gap of 15 minutes in his working time). This is not correct.

 

So if the 15 minute break is taken before 5.5 hours into the shift the system is behaving as expected and deducting a dynamic break of only 15 minutes, however, this is not the case if the 15 minute break is taken after the 5.5 hous. How do I prevent the full 30 minutes being deducted, if 15 minutes have been taken after 5.5 hours into the shift?breaks

Thanks,

Manon

entry.title: SAP SF EC Time Management
entry.content:
Hi All,

There are 3 groups of employees, such as morning shift employees, noon shift employees, and night shift employees. They have to work in 3 different shifts in the same day. 3 shifts of employees should start work on this date: 01/01/1900.

Group A: Morning Shift 6 AM - 2 PM

Group B: Noon Shift 2 PM - 10 PM

Group C: Night Shift 10 PM - 6 AM

Please explain the process for how to create a work schedule for this.

 

This way I created 3 shift patterns (weekly change) for 21 days with different times
6 am - 2pm
2pm - 10pm
10pm - 6am

What is the further process?

entry.title: Generate unique email adresses during the hire process
entry.content:
Hi, 

I need to generate and pre-populate the new hire's email address field, either during the MPH step or immediately after. Any way to achieve this in IC or via business rules?

Thank you for all help.

entry.title: Offboarding employees from SuccessFactors Microsoft
entry.content:
When employees have no active employment in SuccessFactors - the following fields are altered:
activeEmploymentsCount = 0

emplStatus = T

userStatus = f 

We use AccountDisabled to toggle users active/inactive in Microsoft AD
Switch([emplStatus], "True", "A", "False", "U", "False", "P", "False")

If we change scope to exclude any of these

Scope: 
activeEmploymentsCount NOT_EQUALS 0
emplStatus NOT_EQUALS T
userStatus NOT_EQUALS f

the employee are not included in SuccessFactors to Microsoft Active Directory sync, hence user account in Microsoft Active Directory is still active.

If we change scope and sync users with activeEmploymentsCount > 0 - users without an active employment in SuccessFactors are deactivated. 
However - when we delete users from Active Directory, the sync engine will try to create the deleted users, and deactivate them, and then we cannot delete any users in AD. 

Target Object Actions are Create, Update

What have anyone else done for handling offboarding? 

 

Is there a way to deactivate users without active employment,

entry.title: Can we hide or disable Self-description (introductory text) in Latest People profile?
entry.content:
In Latest People profile that will be released in May 2025, it seems like Introductory text (Self-description) will now be mandatory and enabled by default in the Profile preview (Public profile).

Is there a way to hide this feature to users? Enabling/disabling that feature was possible in Legacy People profile in Configure People profile. That option is not there anymore in Latest People profile.

If an employee adds a self-description that could be inappropriate, it seems like we can't edit it or remove it on his behalf. So being able to disable or hide that feature should still be available in Latest.

entry.title: Trigger notification for deletion action
entry.content:
Hello Group,

I need to trigger notification on delete action. For example, if a job information record has been deleted then I would like to get a notification. Is this possible? Any help on this will be appreciated.

Regards,

Surya

entry.title: bulk upload that contains Chinese characters which is failing as the characters are not recognize
entry.content: I need to do a bulk upload that contains Chinese characters which is failing as the characters are not recognized and the field are mandatory, any solution?

entry.title: Unable to update API Error while applying SuccessFactors OAuth connection API Management policy
entry.content:
Hey Peeps!

What am I trying to do - 

I am trying to set up SuccessFactors OAuth connection API Management policy for a Compound Employee SOAP call so that I don't need to configure any authentication credentials in Cloud Integration. 

 

What have I done till now -

- I copied over the 'SuccessFactors_OAuth2SAMLAssertion' policy template from 'Connect to SAP SuccessFactors' policy package from the Integration Suite Discover portal

 

- I copied the 'Compound Employee API' API from the 'SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central' package from the Integration Suite Discover portal

 - I configured the correct SuccessFactors API server URL, host alias, gave it a custom base path, etc.

- I applied the 'SuccessFactors_OAuth2SAMLAssertion' policy template. It automatically created the 8 policies under the Target Endpoint - Preflow.

 

- I configured the SF User ID, Client ID, Company ID, Authentication Token url and the private key. All this was created from the SuccessFactors Employee central - Manage OAuth Applications transaction

- When I hit update and save the API proxy I get the below error - Unable to update API - An internal error occurred, contact SAP Administrator for Detail

 

The weirdest part is that, I followed the same steps to set up an OData API proxy to SuccessFactors and it works like a charm. I tested it out successfully. 

What do you guys think the issue is? 

Kriba


 ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Edit 1: (08-03-2025)

This error is not particular to the Authentication policy. I am getting this error no matter which policy I add - message logging, basic authentication, even a dummy java script with no code. 

 

 ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Edit 2: (08-03-2025)

I have a sneaky suspicion that this is an error that's happening when copying the CompundEMployee API from the Discover portal. See the below when I copy an odata from the discover portal. When I try to edit the APIs in the API designer, I see all the different entities on the left.  

But when I try to check the compound employee API, I get the error 'No operations defined in Spec!'

But when I go to the Discover portal and search for Employee Central APIs and select Compound Employee and then click on Documentation, I see the full list of Operations

 

So, this definitely is a back-end problem. It's not copying over the operations correctly.

entry.title: Exporting Payment Data in People Analytics
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m wondering if anyone has experience exporting payment data, such as IBAN and account holder information, into a  People Analytics report. If so, could you share how you were able to do this

Any insights or tips would be greatly appreciated!

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: How to edit a field in custom MDF when workflow is in progress?
entry.content:
Hi All,

We have a request to update teh comments field as soon as an employee raises the resignation. However, we have workflow configured and the object gets locked. Until the request reached the HR ie approx 45 days from the date of resignation, HR cannot update the comments which they would have captured immediately after the employee raises the resignation. They have to wait for that long. In the mean time, if they forget, the comments will not be captured in the system. Hence wanted to know, can we enable a custom field for the HR to enter the comments though the workflow is in progress and it is independent of the workflow. Kindly help and suggest in this matter.

 

Regards,

Nagavalli B N

entry.title: Replicate employees from EC to ERP HCM who were terminated without notice
entry.content:
Hi everyone

we are currently replicating active employees only from EC to HCM. This is an issue whenever we have cases where the employee is terminated without prior notice. In that case the employee will be made inactive on the same day and thus be already inactive before replication.

Is there any workaround to solve this? I figure there are other companies who come across terminations without notice.

Best regards

Julia

entry.title: In Integration Center, need help in filtering the payload on a field
entry.content:
Hello All,

In Integration Center, trying to filter an email field with 2 conditions - convert it to lower case and then only fetch those who ends with - '@---.com' (here --- can be a company name).

Have tried using a calculated field and calculated filter but not able to find the solution for this, can anyone in the community know the solution for this?

entry.title: Document data request not appearing under "Workflow Details" screen
entry.content:
hi everyone, 

in workflow detail, requestdocumentchild data not appearing in approver screen? how do I can fix the issue??

entry.title: In probation user should only be able to use certain leaves, rest can be used after probation.
entry.content:
Hi experts!

The scenario that I have is, users are credited 30 days of leave, but, the ones who are still in probation can only use 3 days, only after completing the probation, they can use the rest.

Your support would be of great help.

entry.title: SAP SUCCESSFACTORS EMPLOYEE CENTRAL
entry.content:
Bonjour à tous,

Je commence avec SAP et je souhaiterais que quelqu'un puisse m'aider davantage sur ce module en me formant 1h à 2h par semaine. Nous pourrons échanger sur les modalités.

Si cela est possible pour vous, je pourrai vous contacter par mail.
Merci et belle continuation,

Marie

entry.title: SAP SF EC Time Management
entry.content:
Hi All,

There are 3 groups of employees, such as morning shift employees, noon shift employees, and night shift employees. They have to work in 3 different shifts with different timings on the same day.

Group A: Morning Shift 6 AM - 2 PM

Group B: Noon Shift 2 PM - 10 PM

Group C: Night Shift 10 PM - 6 AM

Please explain the process for how to create a work schedule for this.

entry.title: How to restrict Visibility(edit or view) of HRIS element depend on effective end date.
entry.content:
Dear community,

EC Job information have History. I want to restrict visibility of element (not field) by end date.

※I do not want to use business rule.

Effective end date < 2023.3.31, 1st and 2nd records should be only view.

Except for Effective end date < 2023.3.31, 3rd record should be edit.

Ex)

1st 2015.1.1~2018.12.31

2nd 2019.1.1~2023.3.31

3rd 2023.4.1~9999.12.31

Have you faced something similar? How would you advise to proceed in order to make element view onlyfor the login user/worker?

 

Regards,

Akiko

entry.title: IAS + ONB (Welcome Email Day 1)
entry.content:
Hi folks!

We have in place the onboardees login managed by IAS including the Provisioning Real Time Sync. So at the moment that the Onboarding Process starts the onboardee is created at IAS Platform. IAS is sending the Welcome Emails and they are able to login to access, so all good.

Question is:

Is there any way to send an additional welcome email once the onboardee becomes into an employee?

Once the onboardee is created into IAS, it has a IAS User Profile and it receives a Welcome Email thanks to:

 

{
   "condition": "$.userType != 'ONBOARDEE'",
   "targetPath": "$.emailTemplateSetId",
   "constant": "943fec28-aaaa-bbbb-cccc-aaee814453c6",
   "scope": "createEntity"
},
{
   "condition": "$.userType != 'ONBOARDEE'",
   "targetPath": "$.sendMail",
   "constant": "true",
   "scope": "createEntity"
},
{
   "condition": "$.userType == 'ONBOARDEE'",
   "targetPath": "$.emailTemplateSetId",
   "constant": "943fec28-xxxx-yyyy-zzzz-aaee814453c6",
   "scope": "createEntity"
},
{
   "condition": "$.userType == 'ONBOARDEE'",
   "targetPath": "$.sendMail",
   "constant": "true",
   "scope": "createEntity"
},

 

So during the "CreateEntity" the email is sent.

The thing is that during the Hiring Process, HR changes the onboardee data like the "username" and the "email" and those actions are replicated to the IAS User Profile (expected behaviour)

Our concerns is that at the moment the onboardee becomes into a employee, there is not another "CreateEntity" event (we can add the transformation, but doesn't work) it's only an UPDATE into the IAS User Profile, so the user (now is an employee) doesn't know the new username or email due to it was changed during the Hiring Process and is not the original one that he/she received as onboardee.

Is there any way to detect when someone change at IAS side from onboardee to employee in order to send another Welcome Emails with the current username value?

Regards,

Jose Alberto Vargas

entry.title: Licensing Question for Retired Employee Central
entry.content:
We are trying to understand Employee Central Functional License:

  • SAP SuccessFactors License Compliance Module Details
  • 2978690 - Differences Between Full and Functional Users for Employee Central License

 

The document mention that :

 

SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central, core HR option, functional use is available only for the following categories of individuals:

 

  • Former employees whose records continue to be maintained;

Which means that they are : User has an active pensioner relationship with the company (is paid a pension or equivalent by the company, or their personal data and claims need to be still updated (after employment termination) for the purpose of outsourcing the execution of pensions, similar claims, or payments post termination).

 

Are Retiree in Employee Central (Retiree in emplstatus/employee profile inactive) will they count as a functional license?  Can you bring clarity how we identify Retiree with an active pensioner relationship in SF from a licensing perspective? Is it all “Retired” employee status employee?  Is it that they are getting a part of their EC profile updated within a certain period? Or that they are being actively replicated to ECC?
